relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Peter and John go to temple prayer at the ninth hour.
A man from birth-lameness sits daily at Beautiful Gate.
He asks for alms from those entering.
Peter and John give him their gaze.
He expects something from them.
No silver or gold, but the name of Jesus Messiah gives walking.
The right hand raises him and strength enters feet and ankles.
He enters the temple walking, leaping, and praising.
The people see praise embodied.
Recognition of the Beautiful Gate beggar becomes astonishment.
He clings to Peter and John as the crowd rushes to Solomon’s Portico.
Peter refuses credit as personal power or piety.
The ancestral Divine glorified Jesus, the handed-over servant.
The Holy and Righteous One was denied.
The Author of life was killed and raised, and the apostles witness.
Trust in the name gives complete wholeness.
Peter speaks to ignorance without contempt.
The prophets’ suffering-Messiah witness is fulfilled.
Turning and returning open wiped-away misalignment and seasons of refreshing.
Messiah Jesus is the appointed one sent for them.
Heaven receives him until restoration of all things.
Moses points to the prophet to be heard.
Refusing that prophet means separation from the people.
Samuel and all the prophets announced these days.
They are children of prophets and covenant, heirs of Abraham’s earth-blessing.
The raised servant blesses by turning people from harmful ways.
